title: Molten Nutty Tunisian Pork Tagine
description: A rich, flavorful, and aromatic one-pan dish showcasing the bold flavors of Tunisia.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large, heavy-bottomed Dutch oven or clay pot over medium-high heat.
Brown the pork cubes on all sides, in batches if necessary, until they are nicely browned and crispy.
Remove the browned pork from the pot and set it aside. Reduce the heat to medium.
Add the sliced onions to the pot and cook until they are softened and lightly caramelized, stirring occasionally.
Add the grated ginger and minced garlic to the pot and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the ground cumin, coriander, cinnamon, turmeric, salt, and black pepper to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chopped almonds to the pot and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the browned pork, prunes, chicken broth, and bay leaves to the pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake the tagine for 2.5 hours, or until the pork is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly. Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
